name: Wakefern
description_short: Wakefern Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union serving employees of Wakefern Food Corporation, ShopRite, and PriceRite stores with mortgages, loans, and deposit accounts.
description_long:
Wakefern Federal Credit Union operates as a cooperative association owned and controlled by its members under a Federal Charter and Federal supervision by the NCUA. The credit union was established to serve a specific employment-based field of membership: associates of Wakefern Food Corporation and ShopRite and PriceRite retail stores. This restricted membership model ensures the credit union's resources are dedicated to serving employees within this corporate family.

The institution offers a range of consumer financial products including mortgage financing through their Owner's Choice Funding program (supporting first-time purchases, new purchases, refinances, and cash-out refinances), signature loans, vehicle loans, and deposit accounts with competitive rates. Members also have access to insurance solutions for home and auto coverage at low member rates. The credit union recently upgraded its online banking platform and provides audio response system access for account management via phone.

Wakefern FCU distinguishes itself through its cooperative, member-owned structure that theoretically prioritizes member benefits over profits. The credit union maintains a physical ATM location at their Wakefern Foods Produce building in Elizabeth, NJ, and provides fee-free access at partner ATM networks (QuickChek and WaWa locations, subject to usage limits). The institution operates under strict federal fair lending compliance and publishes transparent fee schedules and rates information.

However, Wakefern FCU has significant limitations for most consumers. The membership is strictly limited to Wakefern Food Corporation and ShopRite/PriceRite employees—general consumers cannot join. The credit union maintains minimal physical presence with only one owned ATM and limited branch infrastructure. Service appears primarily phone and online-based, with contact limited to two phone numbers and a website. For eligible employees, the credit union offers standard credit union benefits, but for the general public, this institution is inaccessible.
services: Owner's Choice Funding mortgages (purchase, refinance, cash-out options)
Audio response system for phone account access
pros: Member-owned cooperative structure with no profit motive—returns focused on member benefits
Competitive mortgage products including cash-out refinance and first-time homebuyer options
NCUA federal insurance protection on deposits up to $250,000
Low-cost home and auto insurance solutions exclusive to members
Free ATM access at owned location (505 Division Street, Elizabeth, NJ) and partner networks (QuickChek, WaWa)
Recently upgraded online banking platform for modern digital access
Published rate transparency and fee schedule available to members
Federal fair lending compliance and consumer protection oversight
cons: Extremely restricted membership—only Wakefern Food Corporation and ShopRite/PriceRite employees eligible; general consumers cannot join
Minimal physical footprint with only one owned ATM location and no traditional branch network
Limited phone support options (only two phone numbers; one is an audio response system rather than live representatives)
No mention of online account opening or member services portal details on website
Limited service hours and availability information for member support
best_for: Wakefern Food Corporation employees seeking member-owned banking with competitive mortgage rates
ShopRite and PriceRite store associates needing reliable financial services and low-cost insurance
Employee-owners looking for cooperative credit union structure aligned with corporate affiliation
diagnosis: Wakefern Federal Credit Union is exclusively designed for employees of Wakefern Food Corporation, ShopRite, and PriceRite—general consumers cannot become members regardless of creditworthiness or financial need. For eligible employees, the institution offers solid credit union fundamentals including mortgages, loans, and deposit products with member-focused benefits, but the extremely limited physical and phone infrastructure means members must be comfortable with primarily online and audio-response-based banking.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Wakefern — Credit Union in Elizabeth, NJ | CreditDoc
meta_description: Wakefern — NCUA-insured credit union in Elizabeth, NJ. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
